Given the lack of such a statement through late October, plus the commercial/legal disincentives, I put the chance of an official OpenAI/representative AGI announcement before Dec. 31, 2025 at 7%.

As of the Oct. 26, 2025 cutoff, Monad had clearly advanced beyond mere rumor: the official MON airdrop eligibility portal was open from Oct. 14, users had until Nov. 3 to verify/connect wallets, and multiple reports said eligible users would receive MON on mainnet launch day. This is strong evidence that a real token launch + airdrop was planned soon, not just speculative. However, the key condition for YES is stricter: Monad must both launch the token and actually perform the airdrop with swappable/transferable tokens by Nov. 30. On that point, the evidence was less definitive. CoinDesk and other reports said the airdrop itself had not gone live yet, tokens were not yet transferable, and the mainnet launch date would only be announced after the Nov. 3 claim window closed. Several sources described mainnet simply as expected in Q4 / by end-2025, which leaves meaningful probability of a December launch or delay. So the outcome looked more likely than not, but far from locked in by Nov. 30. I therefore put the probability at 70%.

As of the 2025-10-26 cutoff, WHO had not declared any new coronavirus pandemic, and the official materials available instead point to routine surveillance/long-term threat management rather than imminent emergency escalation. The February HKU5-CoV-2 discovery showed zoonotic potential but no known human outbreak; WHO documents through late October discuss COVID-19, MERS, and potential novel coronaviruses as monitored threats, not active pandemic events. The main non-SARS coronavirus signal in 2025 was limited MERS activity in Saudi Arabia (4 cases, 2 deaths), far from pandemic scale. With only about two months left in the resolution window and WHO historically reluctant to use the formal term “pandemic” absent clear, sustained global human transmission, YES looks very unlikely. I would put the true probability below the market’s 4%, but under the platform constraint I submit the minimum allowed 5%.

I estimate a low probability that NASA GISTEMP’s October 2025 global land-ocean anomaly comes in below 1.10°C (1951-1980 baseline). The strongest evidence gathered before the 2025-10-26 cutoff points against YES: (1) a June 2025 NASA/GISS-related report cited May 2025 at 1.07°C, already very close to the threshold; (2) expert analysis from April 2025 (James Hansen/Columbia) projected 2025 temperatures to remain near or above +1.5°C versus 1880-1920 under ENSO-neutral conditions, which is roughly around 1.2–1.3°C on the 1951-1980 baseline—comfortably above 1.10°C; and (3) 2024 was an exceptionally warm year, so the prior for 2025 monthly anomalies staying elevated is high even without a strong El Niño. Since May was only slightly under 1.10°C, a sub-1.10 October is not impossible, but it would require a notable dip relative to the very hot 2024–2025 regime. The Polymarket price near 0.01 is directionally consistent, though I am a bit less extreme for calibration.

As of 2025-10-26, Kansas City looked like a live but no-longer-clear favorite. Preseason market/odds had the Chiefs only as a modest AFC West favorite (~52-53%), not dominant. By mid/late October they were 4-3 after an 0-2 start, roughly tied in the division race, and current reporting indicated they had already lost early head-to-head games to both the Chargers and Broncos, so existing tiebreak position was a real negative. On the other hand, they had rebounded by winning 3 of 4, still had major divisional swing games left (at Denver in Week 11, then home vs Chargers and Broncos late), and their late schedule gave them direct control paths to retake the division. Mahomes/Reid and the Chiefs’ long division-title streak keep them near the top, but schedule-driven regression from last year’s one-score luck plus credible Broncos/Chargers competition keeps them below 50%. Net: slightly less likely than not, about 47%.

By Oct. 26, 2025, the September leg was already a cut, so this resolves YES only if the Fed pauses in October and then cuts in December. That looked very unlikely. Reuters/CME-based reporting had the October 29 meeting priced for an almost certain 25 bp cut (roughly 98%-near-100%), and a Reuters poll had 115 of 117 economists expecting an October cut. Several officials, including Waller and Musalem, publicly leaned toward an October cut. There were some hawkish/cautious voices and data-blackout uncertainty, so an October pause was not impossible, but it appeared to be only a small tail risk. If a surprise October pause did occur, a December cut would still be plausible given softening labor-market concerns and high market pricing for year-end easing, but the very fact of an October pause would likely mean a more hawkish backdrop, reducing the conditional chance of December easing. Net: cut-pause-cut was a low-probability path, around 5%.

Moonshot’s case is not zero, but it looks very unlikely as of the 2025-10-26 cutoff. The strongest direct evidence is that Kimi-K2/Kimi-K2-0905 was only tied for 8th on LM Arena in early September 2025, not near #1. Meanwhile the top of the field appears to be occupied by major frontier labs such as Google, OpenAI, Anthropic, and xAI, with evidence of continued rapid releases through 2025 (e.g. Gemini 2.5 Pro taking #1 earlier in the year, GPT-5 apparently topping rankings by August in secondary sources, Grok 4/Claude/Gemini all remaining highly competitive). With only about two months left until resolution, Moonshot would likely need a major new release plus fast Arena adoption to jump from top-10-but-not-top-tier to outright leader. That can happen in this space, but it is uncommon and Moonshot does not seem to have the same observed release momentum or resource base as the leading incumbents. Alphabetical tiebreak slightly helps Moonshot against OpenAI/xAI but hurts against Google/Anthropic. Net: small but real upset chance, around 7%.

October 2025 looked likely to stay very warm, but the 1.10–1.14°C GISTEMP bin seemed somewhat below the most likely level. Evidence available by 2025-10-26 showed: (1) September 2025 was still the 3rd-warmest on record in Copernicus and only slightly below September 2024; (2) global SSTs remained persistently high; (3) ENSO was neutral but drifting toward weak La Niña, which argued for some cooling pressure into OND 2025, though not a sharp drop. My best estimate is that October 2025 GISTEMP was centered a bit above this bracket (roughly high-1.1s/low-1.2s), so the named 0.05°C bracket had a modest but not dominant chance. Hence 14%.

By Oct. 26, 2025, the evidence overwhelmingly favored an exactly 25 bps cut at the Oct. 28-29 FOMC meeting. Reuters reported futures pricing around 95%-98% odds of a quarter-point cut, and a Reuters economist poll found 115/117 expecting a 25 bps cut, with the remainder also effectively aligned on October easing (search_0_summary, search_2_summary). The Fed had already cut 25 bps in September to 4.00%-4.25%, and September projections pointed to two more cuts over the remaining 2025 meetings, consistent with another 25 bps in October (search_1_summary). Multiple Fed officials, including Waller, explicitly supported a 25 bps cut, while Powell said the data gap from the shutdown did not materially change the outlook (search_1_summary, search_2_summary). The main residual uncertainty is a surprise hold due to inflation caution or, less likely, a larger 50 bps cut pushed by a dovish minority. Given those tail risks, I cap below certainty but still assign a very high probability.

By Oct. 26, the evidence strongly pointed to Mamdani running first, not second. Multiple late polls had him well ahead in the 3-way field: roughly Mamdani 43-52%, Cuomo 28-33%, Sliwa 13-19% (search_0_summary, search_1_summary). Reporting on Oct. 25-26 described the race as static, with Mamdani ‘well ahead,’ Cuomo second, and Sliwa third; Sliwa was still refusing to drop out, which kept the anti-Mamdani vote split (search_2_summary). Even in a hypothetical 2-way race, Mamdani still led Cuomo 44.6-40.7, so Cuomo needed both consolidation and a polling/turnout break. Early voting had already begun, reducing the time for a major reversal. So YES requires a meaningful late swing or polling miss big enough for Cuomo to overtake Mamdani; that seems possible but unlikely. I put Mamdani’s chance of finishing exactly second at 7%.

Le Havre looked like one of Ligue 1’s weakest sides entering 2025-26 and had not materially improved by the 2025-10-26 cutoff. Preseason market pricing had them at roughly +200,000 to win the title, while Opta projected them 16th with only 33.5 points and major relegation risk, not title contention. By late October they were around 14th, had won just once, had scored only 8 and conceded 10, and reporting framed their season as avoiding relegation while PSG/Marseille led the race. On the merits their true title chance is effectively near zero, but under the required 5%-95% submission bounds I submit the floor value.

Walter Reeves appears to have been an early prospective challenger, but multiple later pre-cutoff sources listing the finalized/qualified 2025 Atlanta mayoral candidates do not include him. Official qualifying closed in August, and reporting in August-October consistently names other candidates while omitting Reeves, implying he likely did not qualify for the ballot. Combined with incumbent Andre Dickens’ strong position and the historical strength of Atlanta incumbents, Reeves’ win chances are extremely low. I assign the minimum allowed 5% probability.

xAI had a real path: Grok-3 reached #1 on Chatbot Arena in Feb/Mar 2025, showing xAI can briefly lead. But by the Oct. 26, 2025 cutoff, the competitive frontier looks crowded and fast-moving, with external comparisons more often highlighting GPT-5, Gemini 2.5 Pro, and Claude-family models as strongest overall rather than Grok as the clear leader. I found no solid pre-cutoff evidence that xAI was still #1 in late October. With only ~2 months to resolution, xAI still has some upside from a new Grok release or leaderboard jump, but it also faces strong rivals from OpenAI/Google/Anthropic and loses any exact tie on alphabetical order. Net: plausible but clearly an underdog, around 11%.

By Oct. 26, the September leg was already a cut, so this outcome required both remaining meetings to be pauses. But available pre-cutoff evidence strongly pointed the other way: Reuters on Oct. 24 said futures implied near-100% odds of an October 25 bp cut and 95% odds of another December cut; a Reuters poll found 115/117 economists expected an October cut, and the September dot plot/minutes pointed to roughly 50 bps more easing by year-end. Since the needed path is pause in October and pause again in December, while consensus expected cut-cut, YES looks extremely unlikely. I would go below 5% absent the platform floor, so I submit the minimum 0.05.

As of 2025-10-26, there is strong evidence of severe escalation—U.S. military buildup in the Caribbean, repeated U.S. kinetic strikes on boats off Venezuela’s coast, unsafe military air/naval encounters, and reporting that Trump was considering strikes inside Venezuela. But the available credible reporting still does not clearly show a qualifying engagement with Venezuelan military forces yet: the struck boats were described as suspected drug-trafficking vessels rather than FANB/coast guard units, and overflights/close approaches do not qualify by themselves. So the key question is the chance of a qualifying incident in the final 5 days before Oct. 31. The short remaining window pulls the probability down, but the very high operational tempo and explicit discussion of strikes on Venezuelan territory keep it materially above baseline. Net: roughly a one-in-three chance of a qualifying U.S.–Venezuela military engagement before the window closes.

My estimate is 18%. As of 2025-10-26, the U.S. had already escalated sharply around Venezuela: multiple lethal strikes on vessels, a large Caribbean buildup (warships, submarine, F-35s, roughly 6,500 troops), CIA covert authorization, and late-October deployment of the USS Gerald R. Ford carrier group. Trump also signaled that operations against cartels 'on land' could come next, and some reporting suggested planners believed they could seize limited facilities such as ports or airfields (search_1_summary; search_2_summary).

But the market resolves YES only if the U.S. begins an offensive intended to establish control over some Venezuelan territory, not merely strike boats, conduct covert action, or launch punitive missile raids. On that key point, most credible expert assessments still said an invasion was unlikely and that the deployed force was not sized for a true invasion/occupation; it was better suited for coercion, stand-off strikes, and possibly small special operations (search_0_summary; search_2_summary). Trump’s political coalition also includes strong anti-invasion instincts, and the legal/diplomatic costs of openly seizing Venezuelan territory would be high.

So the risk was materially above the 10% market print from Oct. 16 because of clear late-October escalation, but still well below even odds. The most likely path remained continued strikes/covert pressure rather than a territorial incursion that would satisfy resolution.

By the Oct. 26, 2025 cutoff, the strongest signal is that no consensus-acknowledged qualifying direct Israel-on-Iran or Iran-on-Israel strike had occurred since the June ceasefire, despite some disputed first-day incidents. The ceasefire was always fragile, and Reuters/CRS reporting shows both sides kept threatening renewed action, with Israel explicitly saying it would strike again if threatened and Iranian officials warning of retaliation. But the key forecasting fact is persistence: after roughly four months without a clearly market-resolving direct aerial exchange, the remaining horizon to Oct. 31 is only five days. I found rhetoric and strategic motives for escalation, but no strong evidence of an imminent direct strike in late October. Proxy/covert activity and broader regional instability do not count unless a missile/drone/air strike impacts the other side’s territory or embassy/consulate. Starting from the Oct. 16 market baseline of 6%, the shorter remaining window and lack of fresh imminent-trigger reporting push me slightly lower, but the floor rule keeps me at 5%.

By 2025-10-26, the US had already carried out major airstrikes on Iranian nuclear sites, but all credible reporting pointed to a limited campaign rather than an effort to seize or hold Iranian territory. Reuters reporting indicated the US told Iran the June strikes were the extent of its plans and did not aim for regime change; Trump was described as unwilling to bear the costs of a regime-change project, and officials repeatedly stressed no boots on the ground (search_1_summary). US force posture in the region was reinforced mainly with air assets and defensive deployments, not the kind of ground mobilization normally needed to establish control over Iranian territory; only small advisory personnel remained in Iraq as of late October (search_2_summary). The resolution bar is high: not just attacking Iran, but commencing an offensive intended to establish control over some part of it. With only about two months left in 2025 and no visible preparation for an occupation-style operation, that outcome looked very unlikely. I leave a small tail risk for extreme escalation scenarios—e.g., a Hormuz crisis or a raid/seizure of an island or coastal position—but overall this was near the floor.

The Weeknd looks very unlikely to finish as Spotify’s official top artist for 2025. Relevant pre-cutoff evidence points to him trailing the leaders: Spotify’s official 2024 Wrapped had Taylor Swift #1 and The Weeknd #2 with Swift at 26.6B streams (search_1_summary), while independent 2025 stream tracking had The Weeknd only #3 and around 10B+ streams, far behind Bad Bunny (~21.0B) and Taylor Swift (~20.6B) (search_2_summary). On top of that, Taylor Swift released a massive new album in October 2025 that broke Spotify single-day and single-week records, further hurting The Weeknd’s chances late in the year (search_4_summary). With only a short time left before Wrapped and The Weeknd apparently well behind at least two rivals, YES is close to the floor. I assign 5% rather than lower only because unofficial trackers can differ from Spotify’s final methodology.

By 2025-10-26, Polymarket had already secured the key regulatory path for a U.S. return: it acquired the CFTC-licensed QCEX/QCX DCM and clearing infrastructure, received a Sept. 3 CFTC no-action/approval pathway for event contracts, and filed self-certifications on Sept. 30 stating products could list no earlier than Oct. 2. That makes a 2025 launch much more likely than not. The main bearish evidence is timing slippage: multiple reports through Oct. 23-24 still described the U.S. relaunch as upcoming rather than already live, and the Oct. 1 government shutdown appears to have slowed CFTC-related operations. Still, the question only requires one publicly reported real-money trade by Dec. 31, 2025, leaving over two months after the cutoff. Given the advanced regulatory status, strong company intent, and relatively modest threshold for resolution, I put the chance high but below the market-implied near-certainty.

By the 2025-10-26 cutoff, the US had already struck Iran from the air in June, but credible reporting indicated no US ground entry into Iranian territory. Subsequent reporting pointed to a ceasefire, renewed diplomacy, and explicit administration messaging against regime change or ‘boots on the ground.’ The resolution criteria are also narrow: regular US military personnel must physically enter Iranian land for combat, while advisors/contractors/SOF do not count. With only about two months left in the resolution window and no clear evidence of preparations for a conventional ground incursion, YES would require a major escalation from the observed pattern of standoff strikes and deterrence. Still, the region remained volatile, so a small residual risk remains.

As of 2025-10-26, A Minecraft Movie’s finalized 3-day domestic opening is about $162.8M, and all already-released 2025 films are below that (e.g. Lilo & Stitch ~$146M, Superman ~$125M, Fantastic Four ~$118M). The main remaining threats are Wicked: For Good, Zootopia 2, and Avatar: Fire and Ash. But the available pre-release evidence is mixed: Wicked was tracking around a potential ~$155M+ opening, close but still below Minecraft; Zootopia 2 had some sensational higher numbers circulating, but more relevant 3-day Thanksgiving tracking appeared more like ~$115-130M with holiday-frame confusion; Avatar is a huge film overall, but the Avatar franchise is historically leggy rather than ultra-frontloaded, with The Way of Water opening at $134.1M. So Minecraft looks more likely than not to finish #1 for opening weekend, though there is still meaningful risk from Wicked and a smaller risk from Zootopia/Avatar.

Google looks more likely than not to finish 2025 on top of the Chatbot Arena text leaderboard, but far from certain. Evidence before the 2025-10-26 cutoff suggests Gemini 2.5 Pro had been #1 on the relevant LMArena text leaderboard at least into early September, despite GPT-5 briefly topping some Arena-related rankings after launch in August. That implies Google was at worst very near the top, and possibly still leading, on the exact resolution source. Google also seems unusually strong on Arena-style human preference evals, with multiple sources noting Gemini’s sustained leaderboard performance. Against that, the benchmark is volatile and a 2-month horizon is long enough for OpenAI, Anthropic, xAI, or Google itself to ship new models that reshuffle the top. Because any Google-owned model counts, Google has both incumbent advantage and multiple shots on goal, but competition remains intense. Net: roughly two-thirds chance.

By 2025-10-26, there is no WHO pandemic declaration in 2025, so this can only resolve YES if WHO makes such an announcement in the final ~2 months. That looks very unlikely. WHO’s formal mechanism is usually PHEIC rather than ‘pandemic’; WHO itself noted during COVID that the PHEIC was the more important legal step. The main candidate threats by late 2025 do not appear close to a WHO pandemic declaration: mpox was still being handled as a PHEIC rather than a pandemic, with spread but not clear worldwide uncontrolled transmission; H5N1 human cases were serious but still limited/localized; chikungunya and dengue were under surveillance/response but not framed by WHO as pandemic declarations. So the remaining probability is only a small tail risk from a rapid deterioration of an existing outbreak or a genuinely new pathogen emerging and spreading fast enough for WHO to explicitly declare a pandemic before year-end.

As of 2025-10-26, Starmer is clearly weakened but still more likely than not to survive the remaining ~2 months of 2025. Evidence for risk: Labour is polling badly (around 10 points behind Reform in some reporting), Starmer’s approval has collapsed, he has faced major internal rebellions over welfare, Reuters reported heavy Labour defeat in Wales on Oct. 24, and leadership speculation around figures like Andy Burnham has grown. The election of a more critical deputy leader also signals unrest. 

But the case against a near-term exit is stronger: Reuters and the NYT both described him as under no immediate threat; Labour still has a huge Commons majority (~172 seats), so there is no realistic parliamentary route to his fall; formal Labour leadership rules require substantial MP backing; likely rivals are procedurally or politically constrained (e.g. Burnham is not an MP); and even reports of internal plotting often pointed to a longer 2026 horizon, not an imminent 2025 removal. With only a short period left in the year, a resignation/removal or announced departure is plausible but still a clear minority outcome. Hence about 13% YES.

John Smith was confirmed in the 64-player field, and as a noted heads-up specialist he had some real ex-ante chance, but even a generous prior in this bracket is only a few percent. By the 2025-10-26 cutoff, multiple sources indicated the event had already been completed/filmed in August, with results pages existing on CardPlayer/Hendon Mob and reporting that prediction-market activity had effectively leaked the winner. Against that, the strongest signal in the prompt is that John Smith’s own market was priced at 0.00 on 2025-10-16, which strongly suggests informed traders believed he was not the winner. I therefore put him at the floor-allowed probability rather than literal zero: a small residual chance of data/reporting error or market inefficiency, but overwhelmingly likely NO.

Z.ai looks like a long shot. Evidence before the 2025-10-26 cutoff suggests: (1) Google’s Gemini 2.5 Pro had reached and held the top LM Arena spot earlier in 2025; (2) Z.ai’s GLM-4.5 was reported around August 2025 as roughly 3rd worldwide on Chatbot Arena, strong but not #1; (3) late-2025 commentary on frontier models more often centered on Google, OpenAI, Anthropic, and xAI than on Z.ai; and (4) the forecast horizon is only 5 days, so there is limited time for a non-leading model to overtake unless a fresh Z.ai release rockets to the top. Also, Z.ai is disadvantaged by the market’s alphabetical tiebreak, so it likely needs an outright lead, not a tie. I therefore put Z.ai at a low but nonzero chance: about 6%.

Federal spending evidence available by the 2025-10-26 cutoff points strongly against a >$250b drop in FRED/BEA federal current expenditures from Q4 2024 to Q4 2025. Treasury/CBO data show FY2025 outlays were about $7.0T, up roughly 4% y/y, so spending was still rising overall despite DOGE efforts and one-time education-related savings. The resolution quarter is Oct-Dec 2025 (FY2026 Q1), which had not yet been fully observed, and achieving YES would require a fairly large y/y decline in the specific NIPA current-expenditures measure; many DOGE-style ‘savings’ (capital/project cuts, accounting changes, delayed obligations) may not fully translate into FGEXPND. There is still a small tail risk from a sharp late-2025 policy/accounting swing—especially education/student-loan related reductions or aggressive agency cuts—but based on the data through the cutoff, NO is overwhelmingly more likely.

As of the 2025-10-26 cutoff, The Life of a Showgirl has already done the hard first part: it debuted at #1 and had an enormous opening (4.002M units) and was still at #1 in its third week with 194K units. That gives it a real path to a long run. But 17 straight weeks is an extremely demanding bar: it would need to stay on top until roughly early February 2026, survive normal decay plus all late-2025/holiday competition, and avoid even a single strong challenger. The best comparison is Swift’s own TTPD, which reached 17 total weeks at #1 but only 15 straight, despite a huge campaign. So the chance is clearly above zero, but still low; I put it at about 7%.

DeepSeek looks like a long shot as of 2025-10-26. The best evidence I found says Gemini 2.5 Pro had been sitting at the top of LM Arena earlier in 2025, while DeepSeek’s hoped-for R2 was still delayed through summer due to performance dissatisfaction and chip/training problems. DeepSeek did release V3.2-Exp on 2025-09-29, but Reuters described it as an experimental/intermediate step rather than the next full-generation model. Meanwhile Google, OpenAI, Anthropic, and xAI all remained active with strong late-2025 frontier models, and Arena dynamics tend to favor large commercial labs with lots of deployment/testing. DeepSeek certainly has upset potential and only needs one breakout model before year-end, but overtaking every rival by Dec. 31 appears very unlikely. I’d put it at about a 2-4% true chance, but given the required submission bounds I submit 5%.

Using the contract’s stated window through Dec. 31, 2025, I put this slightly above even. By the Oct. 26 cutoff, BTC had recently made a new ATH above $125k, then suffered a historic flash-crash/liquidation event that took it down to about $104.8k—already within ~4.5% of the $100k barrier. There were still more than two months left, with elevated volatility, macro/trade-war risk, heavy put/hedging demand, and some Binance-specific stress/wick risk. Offsetting that, ETF inflows and institutional demand were strong, and even the biggest recent selloff did not quite break $100k. Net: meaningful but not dominant chance, around 60%.

Musk is a real but small-probability contender. By late Oct. 2025 he had major 2025 influence: leading/embodying DOGE early in Trump’s term, then breaking with Trump, launching the America Party, and remaining central through Tesla/SpaceX/xAI/X. TIME covered him heavily and put him on the 2025 TIME100. But the evidence suggests he had faded from frontrunner status by autumn: Trump, Pope Leo XIV, and AI were more commonly cited as leading candidates, and market/odds commentary had Musk slipping behind them. If TIME wants to capture the Trump-era political story, Trump is the more likely selection; a joint Trump-Musk cover would also not necessarily resolve YES for Musk under these rules unless Musk were listed first. Musk having already been POTY in 2021 is a mild additional headwind. Net: plausible, but only a long shot.

The album is already 3-for-3 at No. 1 as of the Nov. 1, 2025 Billboard 200, after an enormous debut, and the announced late-2025 release slate looked relatively light with no obvious guaranteed blockbuster challenger. Taylor Swift also has precedent for a very long run: The Tortured Poets Department opened with 12 straight weeks at No. 1 and finished with 17 total nonconsecutive weeks. But 16 consecutive weeks is still an extremely high bar. Showgirl would need 13 more uninterrupted weeks, roughly through the Jan. 31, 2026 chart, and long uninterrupted Billboard 200 runs are very rare in the modern era. The album’s weekly units are already falling sharply (338k in week 2, 194k in week 3), so it will likely become vulnerable to any major surprise release, holiday/catalog surge, or strong December/January competitor. Net: clearly possible, but still a low-probability outcome.

As of 2025-10-26, the bar to beat is A Minecraft Movie’s $162M domestic 3-day opening. The strongest industry-style forecast I found, Boxoffice Pro on Oct. 26, had Zootopia 2 at just $95M-$110M for the 3-day, with a bigger $140M-$160M 5-day Thanksgiving frame. That matters because this market uses only Fri-Sun, and a Wednesday launch tends to shift demand out of the 3-day window. Other chatter/tracking was higher ($115M-$130M, and one bullish report as high as $155M-$190M), but the consensus still seemed below Minecraft. Also, Wicked: For Good was tracking even higher than Zootopia 2 on a 3-day basis ($130M-$155M), and Avatar: Fire and Ash remained a later-year tail risk if the market is interpreted literally as “any 2025 movie.” So Zootopia 2 still had some upside path to win, but it needed to materially outperform most tracking and clear an already very high benchmark. I put YES at 9%.

By the 2025-10-26 cutoff, the best evidence points strongly toward Tyler Robinson being treated by law enforcement as a lone actor. Reuters and NBC both reported that no evidence had emerged linking him to any outside group, and the public case details center on Robinson alone: family-facilitated surrender, his grandfather’s rifle, his own alleged texts/Discord messages, and prosecutors charging only him. The FBI did publicly say it was investigating the possibility of accomplices, but that is standard in a high-profile assassination and, as of the cutoff, there was still no concrete reporting of any helper, co-planner, or second shooter from credible outlets. Residual risk remains because there was not yet a definitive official ‘acted alone’ statement, and the FBI was still reviewing visitors, messages, and possible signals. But given the resolution rule (including resolving on what law enforcement most likely believe by 10/31 if no definitive statement), YES is very likely.

OpenAI looks like a real contender but not the favorite as of the 2025-10-26 cutoff. The strongest text-specific evidence points to Google’s Gemini 2.5 Pro still leading the LM Arena Text leaderboard into September 2025, after holding #1 for months, while GPT-5 mainly appears to have topped some broader/default/category views in August rather than clearly displacing Gemini on the relevant text board. OpenAI does have multiple strong models, a history of jumping back to the top, and enough time before Dec. 31 for a new release or retune to overtake. But the field is crowded (Google, Anthropic, xAI, DeepSeek), Arena rankings are volatile, and OpenAI is hurt by tie-breaks against Google and Anthropic. Starting from a position where OpenAI does not appear to be the clear current text leader, I put its chance of finishing Dec. 31 on top at about 18%.

As of the 2025-10-26 cutoff, available reporting shows BTC hit an early-October ATH above $125k, then sold off hard but only to roughly $104.8k on Oct. 10, with subsequent references around $105k-$111k through Oct. 22-25. I found no evidence that Binance or broad-market BTC had already traded at or below $90k during October, so the question mainly reduces to the remaining last ~6 days of the month. From a spot level near $106k-$110k, $90k would require roughly a further 14%-18% drawdown in a very short window. Reuters reported heavy hedging demand and elevated volatility after the Oct. 10 crash, including interest in Oct. 31 downside puts, which raises tail risk; however, the cited strikes were around $95k/$115k rather than $90k, suggesting sub-$90k was a relatively more extreme scenario. Balancing elevated crypto tail risk against the large remaining distance to the barrier and short time left, I estimate a low but nontrivial YES probability of 8%.

By the 2025-10-26 cutoff, Francesca Muñoz had withdrawn from the presidential race on 2025-06-30 and endorsed José Antonio Kast, and she does not appear in October polling, which instead shows a field led by Jara, Kast, Matthei, Kaiser, and others. Since she was no longer an active candidate and had negligible measured support, her chance of ultimately winning the election is extremely low; I floor this at 5% per instruction not to use 0%.

As of 2025-10-26, tensions are clearly high: the U.S. has already conducted multiple kinetic strikes on vessels off Venezuela, deployed substantial forces (and just announced a carrier-group escalation), Venezuela has surged forces and flown military aircraft near U.S. ships, and Trump officials have openly discussed possible strikes on targets on Venezuelan land. That said, the market’s resolution standard is narrower than the headline tension level: confirmed strikes so far appear to have been against alleged smugglers/non-military vessels, not FANB forces, and there is no credible report yet of a qualifying exchange of fire, shootdown, ship-ramming with major damage, or U.S. strike impacting Venezuelan territory. With only about five days left in the event window, the remaining risk is meaningful but still well below 50%. I estimate roughly a 28% chance of a qualifying U.S.–Venezuela military engagement by Oct. 31, 2025.

Valencia’s title chances were already extremely low before the season: preseason projections and betting markets centered the race on Barcelona, Real Madrid, and Atlético, with Valencia viewed as a mid-table / outside-European contender rather than a title challenger (search_0_summary). By 25 Oct 2025, Valencia had fallen to 14th with just 9 points from 10 matches and had just lost 0-2 at home to Villarreal; they were already far behind the leaders and well behind even 3rd-place Villarreal (search_3_summary). Given recent history, squad quality, preseason expectations, and their poor start, a Valencia title win looks only remotely possible. I would go lower if allowed, but under the required 0.05-0.95 range I submit 5%.

By the Oct. 26, 2025 cutoff, Billboard had already reported that Taylor Swift’s 'The Life of a Showgirl' debuted at #1 on the Billboard 200 for the chart dated Oct. 18, 2025, with 4.002 million units (lookup_1; search_0_summary). Since the '0 straight weeks' bracket only resolves YES if the album never reaches #1 (or is not released by Dec. 31, 2025), that outcome is already effectively ruled out. I assign the floor probability only to allow for negligible publication/correction risk.

By the Oct. 26, 2025 cutoff, the best independent evidence points strongly away from Drake finishing #1 in Spotify Wrapped 2025. Officially, Spotify’s 2024 global top artist was Taylor Swift, with Drake only the top rapper, not the overall leader. Pre-cutoff third-party tracking/commentary consistently framed the 2025 race as led by Bad Bunny and/or Taylor Swift, with The Weeknd also a serious contender; Drake was generally not shown as the year-to-date leader and in one Chartmasters-based summary was around #4 for 2025. Drake’s big 2025 headlines were mostly about all-time cumulative Spotify milestones, which are not the same as being the most-streamed artist of the 2025 calendar year. Since the forecast date is already late October—very close to the Wrapped measurement window end—the odds of Drake overtaking the field are quite small. I leave a small residual chance for tracker error or a late surge, but overall YES looks very unlikely.

By 2025-10-26, the only plausible candidates are US allies such as South Korea and Japan. Debate about independent nuclear weapons had intensified, driven by concern over US security guarantees and regional threats, but there was no credible reporting that any eligible ally had withdrawn from the NPT, begun an acknowledged crash weaponization program, or obtained full independent control of an operational nuclear weapon. South Korea still faced major technical and legal hurdles, with cited expert estimates of roughly 4-5 years; Japan was technically closer but still had not taken the political steps that would be hard to hide and would likely trigger severe diplomatic and economic consequences. With only about two months left in 2025, a YES resolution looked very unlikely. I therefore put this at the minimum allowed forecast: 5%.

By Oct. 26, RFK Jr. had not clearly met the market’s bar of publicly announcing a definitive cause. The strongest evidence is the Sept. 22-23 HHS/press coverage around autism, but those reports described ‘progress,’ possible links such as acetaminophen in pregnancy and folate issues, and explicitly noted that causality was not established. Earlier reporting also said the timeline had slipped and the broader autism data effort was still in progress. With only a few days left until Oct. 31, a fresh, unambiguous definitive-cause announcement was still possible but very unlikely. I therefore put YES at the floor 5%.

By the 2025-10-26 cutoff, Peru appears already eliminated from CONMEBOL qualification. Multiple pre-cutoff sources indicate Peru had 12 points after Matchday 17, trailed the intercontinental playoff spot by 5 points with only one match left, and was therefore out of contention (search_0_summary). Independent contemporaneous reporting also says Peru were eliminated after a 3-0 loss to Uruguay on 2025-09-04/05. Under the market’s rules, once it becomes impossible for Peru to win the World Cup, the market resolves No immediately. So the true chance is effectively ~0; I submit 0.05 only because the instructions forbid 0 or 1.

2025 looked extremely unlikely to overtake 2024 by the 2025-10-26 cutoff. Multiple sources had 2025 running only 2nd or 3rd warmest: Carbon Brief (July) said 2025 was on track for 2nd/3rd and was 'very unlikely' to beat 2024; NOAA (August) said it was unlikely to be warmest; NOAA commentary in January gave 5% or less odds even before later data arrived. Quantitatively, 2025 trailed 2024 by about 0.08C for the first half and ~0.10C for Jan-Jul, while September 2025 was only the 3rd-warmest September and ~0.07C cooler than September 2024 in Copernicus data. That implies 2025 likely still had a substantial Jan-Sep deficit versus 2024, meaning Oct-Dec would have needed to be dramatically hotter than the already-record-hot late 2024 to set a new annual NASA GISTEMP record. Given that setup, YES was only a tail risk. I would put it below 5% if unconstrained, but under the required 0.05-0.95 bounds I submit 5%.

xAI looks very unlikely to lead the Chatbot Arena text leaderboard on Oct. 31, 2025. The strongest pre-cutoff evidence points the other way: Grok 3 briefly led in February, but Gemini 2.5 Pro reportedly took a large #1 lead in late March, and multiple July/August/October sources still place Google/OpenAI models ahead of Grok on LMArena text rankings. One late pre-cutoff source specifically says Grok-4-fast was #8 on the Text Arena as of Sep. 21, while being #1 only on Search Arena, which does not count here. With only five days from cutoff to resolution, xAI would likely need a surprise new model plus enough Arena votes to jump to #1. That is possible, but the short horizon, evidence that xAI was not near the top of Text Arena late in the period, and the unfavorable alphabetical tie-break against companies like Google/OpenAI all push this toward the floor.

As of the 2025-10-26 cutoff, Tesla had not yet launched a qualifying unsupervised FSD product. The strongest evidence is that Tesla’s active robotaxi operations in Austin and the Bay Area were still described as supervised, with human safety monitors/drivers in the vehicle, and Musk said only that he expected safety drivers to be removed in parts of Austin by the end of 2025—not by Oct. 31 (search_2_summary; Reuters results in searches 3 and 5). Tesla did open robotaxi access more broadly to the public in Austin in September, but reports still said rides included a human safety monitor, sometimes even in the driver’s seat for highway trips, which would fail the market criteria (search_4_summary). With only a few days remaining until resolution, the base rate for a major autonomy milestone arriving ahead of Tesla’s own updated timeline is very low. I leave a small residual chance because Tesla could abruptly remove safety monitors in a limited Austin service and make it public before Oct. 31, but the evidence overwhelmingly points to 'not yet' rather than imminent launch.

As of 2025-10-26, the Bucharest by-election is set for Dec. 7, 2025, and reporting/polls center on Daniel Băluță, Cătălin Drulă, Ciprian Ciucu, Anca Alexandrescu, and Piedone. Victor Ponta is not showing up in the mayoral candidate lists or polling I found, despite targeted searches, while he had been focused on the 2025 presidential race and had a complicated relationship with PSD. In a single-round race, a late or surprise Ponta entry would need both candidacy and immediate broad support, which looks very unlikely. I keep a small residual chance for Romanian political volatility and incomplete candidate finalization, but overall this is near the floor.

By the Oct. 26, 2025 cutoff, the first leg is already locked in: September was a 25 bp cut to 4.00%-4.25%. The second leg (October cut) looked close to certain: Reuters/economist polling was about 115/117 for a 25 bp cut, and futures were near 95-100% for an October cut. The key question is December. There, the evidence was strongly against a pause: Reuters on Oct. 24 said futures implied about a 95% chance of another cut in December, and a Reuters poll also had most economists expecting cuts in both October and December. There is some residual pause path—Waller said December was 'not guaranteed,' and BofA had only one more 2025 cut, shifted to October—but that looks like a small minority scenario. Multiplying a very high October-cut probability by a low December-pause probability gives a number around 5%; allowing a bit for model uncertainty, I submit 6%.

SOL appears to be trading around $190-$200 in late October 2025, with most of October spent in roughly a $170-$200 range and key resistance near $200-$234. Its 2025 all-time high was about $293, so $350 would require both a rapid ~80% rally from current levels in just the final days of October and a clear break well above the prior ATH. Possible catalysts like ETF-related optimism could help, but even bullish cited scenarios were more like 30-50% upside or targets near $250-$320, still short of $350. The Binance 1-minute-high resolution allows for a brief spike/wick, which prevents this from being literally zero, but given the very short time left and large gap to target, YES looks extremely unlikely.

Bad Bunny looks more likely than not to finish 2025 as Spotify’s top artist, mainly because he appears to have built a large early and mid-year lead from the January release of DeBÍ TiRAR MáS FOToS and his documented #1 daily/weekly artist runs on Spotify. Independent betting coverage in late September still had him as the favorite even after Taylor Swift’s new album announcement, which suggests his lead was meaningful. The main downside is Swift: she was Spotify’s official top artist in 2023 and 2024, and her October 2025 album reportedly set a 2025 single-day Spotify record, so she clearly remained a live threat. But with only about five weeks left until the usual Wrapped/reporting window, a late surge has limited time to overturn a year-long accumulation advantage. I discount the very bullish market price (0.91 on Oct. 16) somewhat for overconfidence and data uncertainty, but still land on a solid favorite for Bad Bunny.

As of the 2025-10-26 Wikipedia revision, Paul Biedermann is still listed as the men's long-course 200 m freestyle world-record holder at 1:42.00 (set in 2009), with no newer ratified or pending ('#') replacement shown. The extra search evidence also points the same way: even at the 2025 World Championships, commentary still treated 1:42.00 as the standing world/championship record, and the event preview described it as one of the few remaining supersuit-era records. That suggests the mark remained well clear of the field through the biggest 2025 long-course meet. Near-term risk is therefore low. It's not zero because world records can be broken at any ratified 50 m meet, but after late October the calendar is also less favorable for long-course record attempts than during the main summer championship season. I therefore put the probability at the allowed ceiling for the first date, then shade down modestly for the later dates to reflect increasing forecast horizon and uncertainty.

Baseline on 2025-10-26 is effectively rank 15 on Wikipedia’s FIDE rankings page (the 2025-10-22 revision shows Hans Niemann at #15 with 2738 in the Top players table). I interpret the question as asking whether his later rank is at least as good, i.e. rank <= 15. The nearby rating cluster is very tight: #15 Hans 2738, #16 Dominguez 2738, #17 MVL 2737, #18 Ding 2738, so even a small rating dip could push him below 15. On the other hand, rank is fairly sticky, several nearby players are often not very active, and the empirical base rate is 0.68. I adjust downward because Niemann was actively playing the 2025 US Championship right before cutoff, which increases near-term volatility and makes a small drop plausible. For 2025-11-02 I keep a slight extra boost because Wikipedia may still conceivably show the October list or only very recently update to November; by 2025-11-25 it should almost certainly reflect the November list. By 2026-01-24 there is more time for rating movement by both Niemann and close competitors, so the probability moves closer to 0.5.

Wikipedia’s infectious-diseases list still showed Human parainfluenza virus infection (croup) without a vaccine entry as of the 2025-10-01 revision, and the prompt says the most recent known status on 2025-10-16 was No. Additional context before the cutoff is strongly negative: CDC (June 2024) says there is currently no vaccine for HPIV, and the research literature only shows early-stage candidates (eg, phase I for an HPIV2 candidate and preclinical/animal-work for HPIV3). That is far from the kind of established vaccine status that would likely flip this Wikipedia row to Yes. Over only 1 week, 1 month, and 3 months, a genuine change is extremely unlikely; I raise the probability only slightly with horizon to reflect extra time for an unexpected breakthrough or a Wikipedia status change.

Wikipedia’s List of infectious diseases (revision 2025-10-01, fetched at cutoff) shows the Human bocavirus infection row with the vaccine field blank, consistent with the background that the latest known status was No. The additional search found no evidence of any approved or near-term HBoV vaccine; the most relevant literature emphasizes virology, diagnostics, and pathogenesis rather than a vaccine pipeline, and broader 2025 respiratory-vaccine reviews do not identify HBoV as an active late-stage candidate. Given the extremely short horizons from 2025-10-26 to the resolution dates, a change from No to Yes is very unlikely. I put the probabilities at the floor for the first two dates and only slightly higher by 2026-01-24 to reflect the longer horizon and therefore greater uncertainty.

As of the 2025-10-26 Wikipedia page, Lilly King is still listed as the women’s long-course 100m breaststroke world-record holder at 1:04.13. The extra search context points the same way: in 2025 the event remained well off the record, with the 2025 World Championships won in 1:05.19 and previews describing the field as not really threatening the 1:04 range. So YES is very likely on all three dates, with only a modest decline over the longer horizon because a surprise long-course swim or intermediate split is still possible.

As of the 2025-10-26 Wikipedia revision, Ariarne Titmus is listed as the women’s long-course 200m freestyle world-record holder at 1:52.23, set 2024-06-12 in Brisbane. The main threat is clearly Mollie O’Callaghan, who swam 1:52.48 in the same race; Summer McIntosh and Siobhan Haughey are also elite contenders, but the record still has a meaningful cushion. Timing matters here: between late October and late January there are relatively few obvious peak long-course record opportunities, and the biggest 2025 long-course championship window had already passed. That makes the short-horizon risk low. I trim below the very high base rate mainly because this is one of the more vulnerable women’s records—recently lowered twice, with an active rival already within 0.25s—and because Wikipedia may reflect a new pending mark quickly even before full ratification. Hence high but gradually declining YES probabilities with horizon.

Wikipedia’s 2025-10-22 revision shows Vaishali at 2473 on the 2025-10-01 women’s list. Using that as the best anchor, a 1% increase means roughly 2498+. That is a very large jump (+25 points) for an already established 2470+ player. One search found that she won the 2025 Women’s Grand Swiss, which is evidence of strong form and some upward potential, but it does not outweigh the fact that her official listed rating was still 2473 as of October. For 2025-11-02, the page will likely reflect the 1 Nov list, so there is very little time for a +25 official jump: near the floor at 5%. For 2025-11-25, the page is still likely to show essentially the same November list, so only a tiny increase from baseline uncertainty. By 2026-01-24 there are a couple more monthly rating updates and some chance of additional rated events, so the probability rises modestly, but a +1% gain is still uncommon.

Wikipedia’s List of infectious diseases (snapshot before the cutoff) shows Ascariasis with a blank Vaccine(s) field, i.e. currently No. Additional pre-cutoff sources indicate Ascaris vaccines remain in research/preclinical stages (mouse models, in-silico/reverse-vaccinology work), and as of 2025 recent helminth vaccine trial activity was focused on hookworm rather than Ascaris. So the chance that Wikipedia would correctly flip to Yes by any of these near-term dates is very low. I increase the probabilities slightly with time to reflect the longer horizon and greater uncertainty, while keeping them low because a genuine developed vaccine by early 2026 still appears unlikely.

Wikipedia’s 'List of infectious diseases' still showed a blank vaccine field for Gonorrhea in the latest pre-cutoff revision available (2025-10-01), which implies No, and the prompt says the most recent known value on 2025-10-16 was also No. That is strong anchor evidence: despite the August 2025 NHS rollout in England of a gonorrhea vaccination program using 4CMenB, Wikipedia had not updated by the 2025-10-26 cutoff. This suggests either editorial inertia or, more importantly, that editors may not view 4CMenB/off-label MenB use as clearly meaning 'a vaccine has been developed for gonorrhea' for this list. Still, credible pre-cutoff sources describe a 'world-first gonorrhea vaccine' rollout, so there is some chance editors add 4CMenB (or similar) after the cutoff. Because the page had already remained unchanged for weeks after the rollout, I keep the near-term probabilities low; they rise modestly with time as there is more opportunity for a Wikipedia update, but remain well below 50%.

The relevant Wikipedia page still listed Enterovirus infection with no vaccine entry as of the 2025-10-01 revision, and the prompt says the most recent known status by 2025-10-16 was No. External evidence shows vaccines exist for the subtype EV-A71 in China, but those vaccines have existed for years without causing the generic Wikipedia entry for Enterovirus infection to flip to Yes. That implies either Wikipedia editors interpret this row as broader enterovirus infection, or the page is simply stable and unlikely to be updated quickly. So a Yes resolution would most likely require either a reclassification/edit on Wikipedia or genuinely new broader vaccine development within a very short window, both of which look unlikely. I therefore assign a very low chance by 2025-11-02, slightly higher by 2025-11-25, and a bit higher again by 2026-01-24 due to more time for editing or new evidence, while keeping the longer-horizon forecast closer to 0.5 as instructed.

As of the cutoff, the relevant Wikipedia page was still on its 2025-10-01 revision, and in the fetched table the pneumonia-related entry visible ('Bacterial pneumonia') had no vaccine listed. The prompt also says the most recent known value for Pneumonia as of 2025-10-16 was No. So the question is not mainly about real-world biomedical progress in the next few weeks; it is mostly about whether Wikipedia changes its classification.

There is real-world evidence that vaccines preventing some pneumonia exist: pneumococcal vaccines have existed for years, and search results show PCV21/CAPVAXIVE was approved in 2024 specifically for prevention of pneumococcal pneumonia in adults. But that actually cuts both ways: because such vaccines already existed and Wikipedia still showed No/blank by the cutoff, this suggests the page is using a stricter syndrome-level interpretation or is simply stale. Either way, that makes a flip to Yes possible but not very likely over short horizons.

So I assign a very low chance of a change within one week, a somewhat higher but still low chance within one month, and a modestly higher chance by late January due to more time for an editor to revise the entry. These probabilities move slightly toward 0.5 with longer horizon, per the uncertainty instruction.

Wikipedia’s FIDE rankings page as of the cutoff still showed Caruana at 2789 on the 1 Oct 2025 list. A 1% increase from that baseline is about 2817, so this question is really asking whether he can reach an official FIDE rating of roughly 2817 by the relevant monthly list. That is a very large jump (+28) for an already elite 2789 player: with K=10, he would need to outperform rating expectations by about 2.8 points over his rated classical games. The evidence I found says he won the 2025 US Championship, but not with the sort of dominant overperformance that would usually generate anything close to +28 in a single month, and he himself said he had not played especially well. So the November dates look extremely unlikely; they mostly depend on the November list and page-update timing, hence only a tiny difference between Nov 2 and Nov 25. By Jan 24 there is more time and Caruana is strong enough historically to get back above 2800, but 2817 is still well above his current official level and likely requires both enough classical games and a very strong run. So I keep all three probabilities low, with a slight increase for the longer horizon.

Wikipedia’s FIDE rankings page as of 2025-10-22 shows Gukesh at 2752 on the 1 Oct 2025 list, after a documented recent slump from his 2794 peak. So the most likely comparison baseline on 2025-10-26 is around 2752, implying a +1% target of roughly 2780. For an elite 2750+ player, gaining ~28 Elo in one rating cycle is rare; even over two additional cycles it remains uncommon, though Gukesh’s youth and historical volatility give him some rebound chance. The 2025-11-02 date is especially hard because Wikipedia may still not have updated from the October list by then. By 2025-11-25 the page likely reflects the November list, but reaching ~2780 by then is still unlikely. By 2026-01-24 there is more time for recovery, so I raise the probability modestly, but it remains below 10%. If the true 2025-10-26 baseline were higher than 2752, these estimates would be too high, not too low.

Wikipedia’s FIDE rankings page as of 2025-10-22 shows Wei Yi at 2754 on the 1 Oct 2025 list. A +1% move from 2754 requires roughly 2781.5, i.e. about +28 Elo. By 2026-01-24 there is more time for multiple strong events before the Jan list, so the chance rises a bit, but a +28 jump still looks rare. Hence very low probabilities, increasing slightly with horizon.
